steps.title: Block the form (pencil)
steps.description: Make sure you're in pencil mode. Lightly sketch a rounded shape — a little wider than tall, slightly asymmetric. Real apples are rarely perfectly round, so don't aim for a perfect circle.

steps.title: Mark the top and bottom (pencil)
steps.description: Still in pencil, add a small inward dip at the top of the outline where the stem will sit, and a shallower matching curve at the bottom. These are placement guides, not final lines.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the outline
steps.description: Switch to ink mode. Trace your apple outline in one confident pass, following the pencil guide. Don't try to match it exactly — your hand will pick the cleanest path. A slight wobble reads as organic.

steps.title: Ink the stem and leaf
steps.description: Still in ink, add a short stem rising from the top dip, tilted slightly to one side. Then sketch a small leaf next to it with two strokes meeting at a point and a central vein.

steps.title: Suggest the stem well (ink)
steps.description: Add a small curved line just inside the top dip to suggest depth where the stem meets the apple. One short stroke is enough.

steps.title: Clean up
steps.description: Switch to the eraser and remove any pencil guide lines that distract from the inked drawing. Leave the ones that feel like nice construction texture if you like — your choice.
